div.p: To the whole body of Christ’s faithful who shall view or hear the present charter, Roger de Quincy, earl of Winchester, constable of Scotland, sends Greeting: I make known to you all that I have given, granted, and by my present charter made firm, for the salvation of my own soul and my predecessors’, to the abbot and convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to their tenants in Preston, that they be quit in perpetuity of suit of my mill of Tranent, and of multure of all manner of grain of the whole land of Preston pertaining to their church of Tranent. That this may obtain the strength of endurance, I have duly caused the present charter to be strengthened with the defence of my seal. Given at Tranent, on the Monday next before the feast of the apostles Simon and Jude, in the year of grace 1250.
